text: exa-agent contents --output /tmp/file still auto-spilled elsewhere at the 48 KiB ceiling, left the requested output path absent, and made the follow-up rg fail; explicit --output should win or the envelope should state why it did not
A docs-probe shell snippet used the installed exa-agent binary and made an unintended live Exa contents call; repo work should have an explicit no-live-call guard or a docs-only fetch helper.
cargo test accepts only one positional test filter, so Wave E focused tests need one combined filter or separate invocations
Delegate Codex Wave F failed before execution because Codex state DB thread lookup discrepancies caused a harness_error; retry should recover cleanly or surface a repair command.

Cargo kept executing a stale exa-agent build-script binary after build.rs changed (the binary lacked the new BuilderId string even after touch); a scoped build-script invalidation helper would avoid confusing overlay validation failures.
Rust 1.96 accepted std::fs::File::lock, but the repo MSRV 1.85 gate only surfaced via all-features clippy; an MSRV compile helper would catch new-stdlib API drift earlier.
A broad 'find .. -name AGENTS.md' in exa-agent-cli traversed too much and hung; repo-oriented guidance should recommend bounded parent-chain checks rather than recursive parent searches.
cargo test accepts only one TESTNAME filter, so the focused compatibility tests require separate invocations or a shared prefix
zsh parsed an unquoted fixture label beginning with === as an operator; shell-footguns guidance could include safe printf-based loop labels.
A malformed orchestration wrapper caused a JavaScript syntax error before shell commands ran; an editor/linter for tool-call source would prevent avoidable retry churn.
Canonical fixture capture hung because a background local server inherited the command-substitution pipe; helper recipes should redirect background server stdout/stderr before returning its PID.

EXA_AGENT_NO_NETWORK=1 blocks the repository's loopback transport tests, so the full suite cannot run under the guard even though it makes no external calls; document/test a local-fixture exemption or separate probe-only invocation guidance.
In zsh, using  as a loop variable shadows PATH, causing subsequent trash/git commands to resolve as missing; shell guidance should call out the reserved parameter name.
zsh treated an unquoted echo ===$f inspection label as an operator and aborted the command; safer inspection helpers or shell-footgun examples should cover this.
cargo test accepts one TESTNAME filter, not multiple bare test names; a helper or shell recipe for serial multi-test selection would avoid this retry.
Broad parent instruction scan short-circuited without usable output; a small parent-walk helper or documented scope would prevent wasted orientation time.
zsh reserves the status variable, causing a simple CLI inspection command to abort; shell guidance should recommend rc for captured exit codes.
Audited Wave 5 no-network test was coupled to deferred websets CSV-import flags absent from the 0.3.0 baseline; a Wave-scoped fixture matrix would have prevented transplant failures
Shell orientation command embedded a multiline printf inside one zsh -c string and failed with unmatched quote; a shell-safe helper for emitting audit notes would have prevented it

evidence.cmd: git status plus skill inventory printf

text: Native review sub-agent spawn failed with 'no thread with id' before execution; a valid collaboration thread or clearer unavailable-state response would have prevented the blocked review lane
Papercuts JSONL inspection piped padded nl output into jq tonumber and failed; a JSONL listing command with built-in line numbers would avoid this parsing footgun
Focused review command passed two cargo test positional filters; cargo accepts one, so the HEAD-snapshot verification aborted before running.
A guessed clap source glob used a nonexistent value_range.rs path and zsh aborted on no match; rg on the builder directory or a quoted glob would avoid this
Focused Wave 5 verification used a plausible no_network test target name, but Cargo exposes no_network_transport; a generated test-target index would have prevented the failed gate attempt.
Unified exec rejected a command whose workdir was to be created by that same command; clearer preflight messaging would prevent this dead-end

evidence.cmd: git worktree add /tmp/exa-agent-main-wave5-fix main && cargo build --release

text: cargo test rejects multiple test-name filters without explaining the one-filter rule; a suggested next command would prevent this retry
Native final-review subagent spawn failed because collaboration could not resolve the active thread ID; a preflight availability check or fallback hint would prevent blocked review orchestration
